buffer_metric: EU27 underground gas storage working gas
demand_denominator: EU natural gas inland demand of 13,093,256 TJ in 2025 (= 3,637.0 TWh/yr)
reporting_cadence_or_lag: Daily inventory reporting; first publish around 19:30 and a second processing run around 23:00, with possible weekend lag and retroactive corrections
notes: GIE homepage showed EU storage at 565.62 TWh and 50.03% full on 2026-07-06 at 06:00 CEST. Use working gas volume, not geological capacity. Average-demand days overstate winter/peak coverage.

buffer_metric: LNG tank inventory / total LNG storage capacity
reporting_cadence_or_lag: Public LNG survey discontinued in April 2022; no regular public stock series
notes: METI said on 2026-03-03 that Japan has LNG stock to cover national consumption for approximately three weeks. IEA gives >18 mcm (~12 bcm) storage capacity, roughly 36 days of domestic natural gas. This is LNG tank inventory, not underground storage.

stock_or_capacity_basis: observed reserve rule + LNG tank capacity
demand_denominator: Average daily domestic sales over the last 24 months
reporting_cadence_or_lag: No public daily stock series in cited sources; policy analysis is the public hook
confidence: High on rule, medium on contemporaneous fill
notes: Korea has no underground gas storage and stores gas as LNG in above-ground tanks. IEA says KOGAS terminals have 74 tanks and 6.56 bcm of storage capacity. The stockholding rule is 7 days mandatory inventory + 30 days preventive reserve.

demand_denominator: 2024 national gas consumption up to 425 bcm
reporting_cadence_or_lag: Annual corporate disclosure; no unified daily public inventory dashboard found
notes: CNPC said 23 storage facilities were operational by end-2024, with 18.43 bcm total gas injection in 2024 and 22.6 bcm cumulative peak-shaving capacity. Practical coverage is lower than the simple days equivalent.

buffer_metric: LNG regasification capacity, not national storage stock
stock_or_capacity_basis: no direct public national storage stock series found
demand_denominator: PIB disruption denominator: 189 MMSCMD total gas consumption; 47.4 MMSCMD affected by force majeure
days_of_average_demand: N/A direct; affected supply equals about 25% of average daily gas demand
reporting_cadence_or_lag: PPAC/official gas consumption data are monthly or event-specific; public national storage inventory series is absent
notes: India's public evidence supports a disruption-rate denominator, not storage days. PIB says two new-source LNG cargoes were en route and the Natural Gas Control Order rationed industry/fertilizer/refining/petrochemicals; any days-of-cover estimate requires a terminal-tank inventory model and operating assumptions.

stock_or_capacity_basis: modeled from SLNG tank nameplate capacity: 3 x 180000 m3 plus 1 x 260000 m3 LNG
demand_denominator: EMA 2024 total natural gas supply of 453040 TJ; conversion assumption 24.2 GJ per m3 LNG
reporting_cadence_or_lag: No public daily tank inventory found; EMA annual energy balance; SLNG nameplate capacities are static
notes: Capacity ceiling only. Actual usable emergency stock is lower because heel, commercial inventory, trading/reload services, and pipeline/LNG supply mix matter. Singapore is included because public tank capacity and annual gas denominator are unusually clean for Southeast Asia.

stock_or_capacity_basis: APERC/APEC modeled emergency exercise, not observed stock
demand_denominator: Stage-1 Qatargas disruption scenario; denominator is affected LNG supply, not total national gas demand
days_of_average_demand: 36 days of scenario affected LNG supply
reporting_cadence_or_lag: Static 2024 exercise report; not a live inventory series
confidence: Medium for scenario; low for current inventory
notes: Use only as a supported Southeast Asia stress-test case. It should not be presented as Thailand having 36 days of whole-economy gas demand in storage.
